entry.name: TAlways
entry.description_fallback: Tri-state boolean prop pattern — forces a value always on, always off, or defers to the component default.
entry.definition: export type TAlways = boolean | 'always'

values.description_fallback: Force the behaviour on.
Force the behaviour off.
Keep the behaviour active unconditionally, even in states that would normally suppress it (e.g. hover-only).

examples.title_fallback: SliderField with ticks always visible vs on thumb only
examples.code:
<!-- ticks only near thumb (default) -->
<origam-slider-field :model-value="50" show-ticks />

<!-- ticks always shown -->
<origam-slider-field :model-value="50" show-ticks="always" />

entry.name: TAudioControls
entry.description_fallback: Controls rendering strategy for OrigamAudio — custom Origam UI or native browser controls.
entry.definition: export type TAudioControls = 'custom' | 'native'

values.description_fallback: Renders the OrigamMediaController shell on top of the <audio> element. The native controls attribute is suppressed. Default.
Passes the native controls attribute to the <audio> element and lets the browser paint its own bar. No custom UI is rendered.

examples.code:
<!-- Custom Origam controls (default) -->
<origam-audio src="/track.mp3" controls="custom" />

<!-- Native browser controls -->
<origam-audio src="/track.mp3" controls="native" />

entry.name: TAudioLoopMode
entry.description_fallback: Tri-state loop strategy for OrigamAudio when a playlist is active.

values.description_fallback: No loop. Playback stops when the last track ends.
Loop the whole playlist. After the last track, wrap back to the first one.
Loop the current track. The same track restarts at 0 when it ends. Prev/next still navigate the playlist.

entry.name: TAudioVariant
entry.description_fallback: Visual variant of OrigamAudio — expanded studio strip or compact transport dock.

values.description_fallback: Full studio strip: 96 px album cover, title/artist/album metadata header, mini waveform scrubber above the transport row.
Slim transport-only dock: 48 px cover, single inline metadata strip, no waveform. Ideal for sticky bottom players.
Deprecated alias for "expanded". Kept for backward compatibility with v0.x consumers.
Deprecated alias for "compact". Kept for backward compatibility with v0.x consumers.

examples.code:
<!-- Full studio strip -->
<origam-audio src="/podcast.mp3" variant="expanded" title="Episode 42" />

<!-- Compact dock -->
<origam-audio src="/podcast.mp3" variant="compact" title="Episode 42" />

entry.name: block
entry.description_fallback: Boolean prop on OrigamBtn that makes the button stretch to fill 100% of its container width.
entry.definition: block?: boolean

values.description_fallback: The button expands to full container width (width: 100%; display: flex).
Default behaviour — the button sizes itself to its content.

entry.name: TBlockquoteAlign
entry.description_fallback: Horizontal alignment of the citation body in OrigamBlockquote.
entry.definition:
export type TBlockquoteAlign =
    | 'left'
    | 'center'
    | 'right'

values.description_fallback: Left-aligned text. Default for all variants except pull.
Centred text. Default for the pull variant.
Right-aligned text.

examples.code:
<!-- centre (default for pull) -->
<origam-blockquote variant="pull" align="center">
  Great things are done by a series of small things.
</origam-blockquote>

<!-- override to left -->
<origam-blockquote variant="pull" align="left">
  Great things are done by a series of small things.
</origam-blockquote>

entry.name: TBlockquoteLang
entry.description_fallback: Locale hint for OrigamBlockquote decorative quote marks — only active for variant="quoted".
entry.definition:
export type TBlockquoteLang =
    | 'fr'
    | 'en'
    | 'es'
    | 'de'
    | 'auto'

values.description_fallback: French guillemets: « … »
Curly double quotes: " … "
Spanish angular quotes: « … » (same glyphs as French, distinct key for future locale tweaks).
German low-9 / high-6 pair: „ … "
Reads the lang attribute from the element or <html lang>. Falls back to "en" when no locale is found. Default.

examples.code:
<!-- French: « … » -->
<origam-blockquote variant="quoted" lang="fr">
  La vie est belle.
</origam-blockquote>

<!-- German: „ … " -->
<origam-blockquote variant="quoted" lang="de">
  Das Leben ist schön.
</origam-blockquote>

entry.name: TBlockquoteVariant
entry.description_fallback: Visual typographic variants for OrigamBlockquote — five self-contained treatments from neutral to editorial.
entry.definition:
export type TBlockquoteVariant =
    | 'default'
    | 'elegant'
    | 'quoted'
    | 'minimal'
    | 'pull'

values.description_fallback: Left accent bar + comfortable padding-inline. Neutral rhythm, fits most prose contexts.
Serif face, italic, more breathing room. Designed for editorial long-form content (essays, articles).
Large decorative open/close marks, locale-aware via the lang prop. Suited for marketing pages and hero quotes.
Bare italic + small indent. Inline citations inside technical docs where the visual must stay quiet.
Pull quote: large body type, centred, extracted out of the flow. Use sparingly — one per article max.

examples.code:
<origam-blockquote variant="default">Default accent bar.</origam-blockquote>
<origam-blockquote variant="elegant">Elegant serif italic.</origam-blockquote>
<origam-blockquote variant="quoted" lang="en">Decorative quote marks.</origam-blockquote>
<origam-blockquote variant="minimal">Minimal inline citation.</origam-blockquote>
<origam-blockquote variant="pull">Pull quote — large and centred.</origam-blockquote>

entry.name: TBottomNavPosition
entry.description_fallback: Horizontal alignment of the bottom navigation bar when it does not span the full width.

values.description_fallback: Aligns the bar to the inline-start edge (left in LTR).
Centers the bar horizontally within the layout region.
Aligns the bar to the inline-end edge (right in LTR).

examples.title_fallback: Bottom navigation centered with custom width

entry.name: TBracketVariant
entry.description_fallback: Tournament bracket topology: single-elimination tree, double-elimination with winners/losers brackets, or round-robin matrix.

values.description_fallback: Classic knockout tree — one loss and you're out. Rounds are laid out as columns from earliest to final.
Two parallel brackets (winners + losers) with a grand-final round. Rounds segregated by IBracketRound.side.
NxN matrix where every team plays every other team. Replaces the tree layout with a grid.

entry.name: TBreadcrumbItem
entry.description_fallback: Shape of an item in the Breadcrumb items array — either a plain string label or a partial props object for full control.

examples.title_fallback: Object items with links and disabled state
examples.code:
<origam-breadcrumb
  :items="[
    { title: 'Home',     to: '/' },
    { title: 'Products', to: '/products' },
    { title: 'Shoes',    disabled: true },
  ]"
/>

entry.name: TCalendarNavigate
entry.description_fallback: Navigation direction for the calendar toolbar and the navigate event: move to the previous period, next period, or jump to today.
entry.definition: export type TCalendarNavigate = 'prev' | 'next' | 'today'

values.description_fallback: Moves the calendar back by one period (month, week, or day depending on the active view).
Advances the calendar forward by one period.
Jumps the calendar to the current date regardless of which period is currently displayed.

entry.name: TCalendarTimeFormat
entry.description_fallback: Clock format for time labels in week, day, and agenda views: 24-hour (00:00–23:59) or 12-hour with AM/PM.
entry.definition: export type TCalendarTimeFormat = '12h' | '24h'

values.description_fallback: 24-hour clock — 00:00 to 23:59. Default format for all locales except en-US.
12-hour clock — 12:00 AM to 11:59 PM. Honours locale-specific AM/PM labels.

entry.name: TCalendarView
entry.description_fallback: Calendar display mode: month, week, day, or agenda.
entry.definition: export type TCalendarView = 'month' | 'week' | 'day' | 'agenda'

values.description_fallback: 6×7 day grid — the canonical month-at-a-glance view.
7-column vertical timeline with one row per time slot.
Single-column vertical timeline zoomed into one day.
Flat list grouped by day — optimal for screen readers and dense schedules.

entry.name: TCardType
entry.description_fallback: Layout variant for a Card used inside a collection: grid (fixed-size tiles) or list (full-width rows).

values.description_fallback: Fixed-size tile layout — all cards have equal dimensions and are arranged in a CSS grid.
Full-width row layout — cards stack vertically and span the container width.

examples.title_fallback: Card in grid vs list layout
examples.code:
<origam-card type="grid" title="Grid card" text="Fixed tile" />
<origam-card type="list"  title="List card" text="Full-width row" />

entry.name: TChartCartesianKind
entry.description_fallback: Cartesian chart topology accepted by OrigamChartCartesian — a narrowed subset of TChartType covering only X/Y axis visualisations.
entry.definition:
export type TChartCartesianKind =
    | 'line'
    | 'area'
    | 'bar'
    | 'column'
    | 'scatter'
    | 'spline'
    | 'stepped-line'

values.description_fallback: Connects data points with straight line segments using the linePath generator. Optional smoothing via the smoothing prop.
Filled area beneath the line — areaPath (fill) combined with linePath (stroke) per series.
Horizontal rectangles — one <rect> per data point along the Y axis.
Vertical rectangles — one <rect> per data point along the X axis (classic bar chart orientation).
Individual circles at data coordinates — no connecting path between points.
Smooth curved line — linePath forced to monotone smoothing. Equivalent to line with smoothing="monotone".
Staircase polyline with right-angle segments — useful for step-function data.

entry.name: TChartLegendPosition
entry.description_fallback: Anchor position of the legend block inside a chart — the plotting area shrinks or grows to accommodate the legend on the chosen side.
entry.definition: export type TChartLegendPosition = 'top' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'right'

values.description_fallback: Legend rendered above the plotting area, items arranged in a horizontal row.
Legend rendered below the plotting area (default for most chart families), items in a horizontal row.
Legend rendered to the left of the plotting area, items stacked in a vertical column.
Legend rendered to the right of the plotting area, items stacked in a vertical column.

examples.title_fallback: Chart with legend on the right side

entry.name: TChartSmoothing
entry.description_fallback: Path interpolation strategy for line, area and spline charts — controls how segments are drawn between consecutive data points.
entry.definition: export type TChartSmoothing = 'none' | 'curve' | 'monotone'

values.description_fallback: Straight line segments between data points. Accurate to the raw values; no overshoot at peaks.
Cubic Bezier interpolation using Catmull-Rom tangent estimation. Visually smoother but may slightly overshoot sharp peaks.
Monotone cubic interpolation (Fritsch-Carlson). Smooth like curve but guaranteed not to overshoot — the implicit default for type="spline".

entry.name: TChartStacking
entry.description_fallback: Stacking strategy for bar, column and area charts — controls whether series are stacked by absolute values or normalised to 100 %.

values.description_fallback: Series are stacked using their raw absolute values. The Y-axis spans 0 → max(stack total).
Each stack is normalised to 100 %. The Y-axis is fixed at 0 → 100 and tick labels default to the "${v}%" format. The tooltip shows both the raw value and its share.

examples.code:
<origam-chart-cartesian
  type="column"
  stacking="percent"
  :series="[
    { name: 'Product A', data: [40, 60, 80] },
    { name: 'Product B', data: [60, 40, 20] },
  ]"
  :categories="['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3']"
/>

entry.name: TChartType
entry.description_fallback: Union of all visualisation primitives accepted by OrigamChart — selects the topology and path generator at runtime.
entry.definition:
export type TChartType = `${CHART_TYPE}`

// Where CHART_TYPE is:
export enum CHART_TYPE {
    LINE         = 'line',
    AREA         = 'area',
    BAR          = 'bar',
    COLUMN       = 'column',
    PIE          = 'pie',
    DONUT        = 'donut',
    SCATTER      = 'scatter',
    RADAR        = 'radar',
    SPLINE       = 'spline',
    STEPPED_LINE = 'stepped-line',
    GAUGE        = 'gauge',
    PYRAMID      = 'pyramid',
    FUNNEL       = 'funnel',
    HONEYCOMB    = 'honeycomb',
    TREEMAP      = 'treemap',
    SANKEY       = 'sankey',
    WORD_CLOUD   = 'word-cloud',
    HEATMAP      = 'heatmap',
    SUNBURST     = 'sunburst',
    BOX_PLOT     = 'box-plot',
    PICTORIAL    = 'pictorial',
    CANDLESTICK  = 'candlestick',
    STREAMGRAPH  = 'streamgraph',
    VARIWIDE     = 'variwide',
    POLAR_BAR    = 'polar-bar',
    BULLET       = 'bullet',
    PARETO       = 'pareto',
    MAP          = 'map',
    SPARKLINE    = 'sparkline'
}

values.description_fallback: Connects data points with line segments. Supports smoothing prop.
Filled area beneath the line — good for cumulative or part-to-whole data.
Horizontal rectangles — one per data point along the Y axis.
Vertical rectangles — classic bar chart orientation along the X axis.
Circular segments proportional to each series value. No inner radius.
Like pie but with a hollow centre — centre slot can display a summary.
Individual circles at data coordinates — no connecting path.
Spider / web chart — each axis radiates from the centre.
Smooth curved line — equivalent to line with smoothing="monotone".
Staircase polyline with right-angle segments — for step-function data.
Semicircular arc dial showing a single KPI value with min/max range.
Stacked trapezoids widening downward — hierarchy or funnel metaphor.
Narrowing trapezoids from top to bottom — conversion pipeline stages.
Hexagonal tile grid — categorical or heatmap colour mode.
Nested rectangles sized by value — hierarchical data at a glance.
Flow diagram with weighted links between nodes — energy or traffic flows.
Text tags sized by frequency — qualitative corpus analysis.
Matrix of colour-encoded cells — two-dimensional correlation or density.
Radial partition chart — hierarchical data as concentric arcs.
Box-and-whisker plot — median, quartiles and outlier distribution.
Icon / silhouette filled proportionally to a value — infographic-style.
OHLC chart — open/high/low/close per period for financial data.
Stacked area chart centred around a flowing baseline — temporal flow.
Column chart where column width encodes a second dimension of data.
Bars arranged radially around a polar axis — cyclical comparisons.
Compact horizontal bar with target marker and performance bands.
Column chart with cumulative percentage line — 80/20 analysis.
Choropleth map — geographic regions coloured by metric value.
Micro-chart — axis-less, label-less trend line for inline KPI context.

examples.code:
<origam-chart
  type="donut"
  :series="[
    { name: 'Vue', data: [65] },
    { name: 'React', data: [25] },
    { name: 'Angular', data: [10] },
  ]"
/>

entry.name: TCodeLang
entry.description_fallback: Syntax-highlighting language identifiers supported by OrigamCode — a curated subset of shiki grammars bundled with the design system.
entry.definition:
export type TCodeLang = `${CODE_LANG}`

// Where CODE_LANG is:
export enum CODE_LANG {
    PLAINTEXT = 'plaintext',
    VUE       = 'vue',
    TS        = 'ts',
    JS        = 'js',
    TSX       = 'tsx',
    JSX       = 'jsx',
    SCSS      = 'scss',
    CSS       = 'css',
    JSON      = 'json',
    BASH      = 'bash',
    HTML      = 'html',
    XML       = 'xml',
    YAML      = 'yaml',
    MD        = 'md'
}

values.description_fallback: No syntax highlighting — raw text output.
Vue SFC — template, script and style blocks highlighted.
TypeScript — type annotations, generics and interfaces.
JavaScript — ES2022+ syntax supported.
TypeScript JSX — React TSX files.
JavaScript JSX — React JSX files.
SCSS — variables, nesting and mixins highlighted.
CSS — properties, selectors and at-rules.
JSON — keys, values and nested structures.
Bash / shell script — commands, flags and pipes.
HTML — tags, attributes and inline scripts/styles.
XML — elements, attributes and CDATA sections.
YAML — keys, values, anchors and multi-line strings.
Markdown — headings, emphasis, links and code fences.

entry.name: TColor
entry.description_fallback: Accepted value for color/bgColor/textColor props — intent name, raw CSS color, gradient string or structured gradient object.
entry.definition: export type TColor = string | IGradient | false | null | undefined

entry.name: TChartHoneycombColorMode
entry.description_fallback: Colour encoding mode for the OrigamChartHoneycomb component — selects whether cells are painted by category or by a continuous heatmap scale.

values.description_fallback: Each hexagonal cell is painted with the intent/series colour of its category. Default mode.
Cells are colour-interpolated along a two-stop gradient driven by the numeric value — from cold (low) to hot (high).

examples.title_fallback: Honeycomb chart in heatmap colour mode

entry.name: TCols
entry.description_fallback: Column span value for OrigamCol — accepts a numeric 1–12 grid column count, "auto" (content-sized), or true (fill remaining space).
entry.definition:
export type TCols = `${COLS}` | true | 'auto'

// Where COLS is:
export enum COLS {
    ONE    = 1,
    TWO    = 2,
    THREE  = 3,
    FOUR   = 4,
    FIVE   = 5,
    SIX    = 6,
    SEVEN  = 7,
    EIGHT  = 8,
    NINE   = 9,
    TEN    = 10,
    ELEVEN = 11,
    TWELVE = 12,
}

values.description_fallback: Spans 1 column out of 12 — approximately 8.33 % of the row width.
Spans 2 columns — approximately 16.67 % of the row width.
Spans 3 columns — one quarter of the row (25 %).
Spans 4 columns — one third of the row (33.33 %).
Spans 5 columns — approximately 41.67 % of the row width.
Spans 6 columns — exactly half the row width.
Spans 7 columns — approximately 58.33 % of the row width.
Spans 8 columns — two thirds of the row (66.67 %).
Spans 9 columns — three quarters of the row (75 %).
Spans 10 columns — approximately 83.33 % of the row width.
Spans 11 columns — approximately 91.67 % of the row width.
Spans 12 columns — the full row width.
Column shrinks to fit its content — no fixed grid-column span.
Column expands to fill all remaining space in the row (flex-grow: 1 equivalent).

examples.title_fallback: Responsive 3-column layout collapsing to 12 on mobile

entry.name: TCoverPosition
entry.description_fallback: Side of the audio surface where the album cover is painted relative to the controller and metadata column in OrigamAudio.

values.description_fallback: Album cover is rendered on the left side of the audio player layout.
Album cover is rendered on the right side of the audio player layout.

examples.title_fallback: Audio player with the cover on the right

entry.name: TCrossOrigin
entry.description_fallback: CORS mode for cross-origin media requests — mirrors the native crossorigin attribute on ,

values.description_fallback: No CORS request is sent — the resource is fetched without credentials (equivalent to omitting the attribute).
CORS request sent without credentials (cookies, HTTP authentication). The server must respond with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * or the requesting origin.
CORS request sent with credentials (cookies, client certificates). The server must respond with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true and a specific origin.

examples.title_fallback: Image loaded with anonymous CORS for canvas access

entry.name: TDateMode
entry.description_fallback: DatePicker view mode: month (day grid), months (month selector), or years (year list).

values.description_fallback: Day grid — shows the current month with individual day cells.
Month selector — shows a 3×4 grid of months for quick month picking.
Year list — shows a scrollable list of years.

entry.name: TDensity
entry.description_fallback: Vertical spacing density: default, compact, comfortable.

values.description_fallback: Standard vertical padding — balanced for most contexts.
Reduced vertical padding — useful in data tables and toolbars.
Increased vertical padding — comfortable for forms and cards.

examples.code:
<origam-text-field density="comfortable" label="Comfortable" />
<origam-text-field density="default"     label="Default"     />
<origam-text-field density="compact"     label="Compact"     />

entry.name: TDirection
entry.description_fallback: Axis direction: horizontal or vertical.

values.description_fallback: Horizontal axis — items laid out left to right.
Vertical axis — items stacked top to bottom.

entry.name: TDisplayLevel
entry.description_fallback: Responsive breakpoint threshold prop — accepts a named breakpoint key or a raw pixel width, below which the component switches to its mobile behaviour.

values.description_fallback: Extra-small breakpoint — typically < 600 px.
Small breakpoint — typically 600–959 px.
Medium breakpoint — typically 960–1279 px.
Large breakpoint — typically 1280–1919 px.
Extra-large breakpoint — typically 1920–2559 px.
Double extra-large breakpoint — typically ≥ 2560 px.
Any integer pixel value used as a custom mobile threshold.

examples.title_fallback: Hide arrows below the sm breakpoint

entry.name: TEmptyStateAlign
entry.description_fallback: Horizontal alignment of the icon, title, description and actions stack inside <OrigamEmptyState>.
entry.definition:
/**
 * Horizontal alignment of the icon / title / description / actions
 * stack inside `<OrigamEmptyState>`.
 *
 * - `center` — default. Centred stack, content also `text-align: center`.
 *              Best for full-page or full-section placeholders.
 * - `left`   — left-aligned stack. Best for narrow side panels or list
 *              empty zones where the surrounding content is left-aligned.
 */
export type TEmptyStateAlign = 'center' | 'left'

values.description_fallback: Default. Centred stack — icon, title, description and actions are all horizontally centred. Best for full-page or full-section placeholders.
Left-aligned stack. Best for narrow side panels or list empty zones where the surrounding content is left-aligned.

examples.code:
<origam-empty-state
  preset="no-data"
  title="No items yet"
  description="Create your first item to get started."
  align="center"
/>

examples.title_fallback: Left-aligned empty state for side panels
examples.code:
<origam-empty-state
  preset="no-results"
  title="No results"
  description="Try adjusting your filters."
  align="left"
/>

entry.name: TEmptyStatePreset
entry.description_fallback: Visual presets for <OrigamEmptyState>. Each preset bundles a default icon and intent so consumers do not need to wire those by hand for the common cases.
entry.definition:
/**
 * Visual presets for `<OrigamEmptyState>`. Each preset bundles a
 * default icon + intent mapping so consumers do not need to wire those
 * by hand for the 95% case. The icon and intent can still be
 * overridden via the matching props when a one-off variation is needed.
 *
 * - `no-data`     — generic "nothing to show yet" (empty collection).
 * - `no-results`  — search or filter returned nothing.
 * - `error`       — an operation failed; retry CTA is typical.
 * - `offline`     — connectivity issue; offline-mode CTA is typical.
 * - `locked`      — feature is gated (auth, plan, permissions).
 */
export type TEmptyStatePreset =
    | 'no-data'
    | 'no-results'
    | 'error'
    | 'offline'
    | 'locked'

// Runtime enum mirror (for iteration in stories / switch blocks):
export enum EMPTY_STATE_PRESET {
    NO_DATA    = 'no-data',
    NO_RESULTS = 'no-results',
    ERROR      = 'error',
    OFFLINE    = 'offline',
    LOCKED     = 'locked',
}

values.description_fallback: Generic "nothing to show yet" placeholder — used when a collection is empty and no search/filter is active.
A search or filter returned no matching items. The CTA typically invites the user to clear or adjust filters.
An operation or data fetch failed. The CTA is typically a retry button.
No network connection detected. The CTA typically offers offline-mode or a reconnect action.
The feature is gated behind authentication, a pricing plan, or permissions. The CTA invites the user to upgrade or sign in.

examples.code:
<origam-empty-state
  preset="no-data"
  title="No projects yet"
  description="Create your first project to get started."
>
  <template #actions>
    <origam-btn color="primary">New project</origam-btn>
  </template>
</origam-empty-state>

<origam-empty-state
  preset="error"
  title="Something went wrong"
  description="We could not load your data. Please try again."
>
  <template #actions>
    <origam-btn color="danger" @click="reload">Retry</origam-btn>
  </template>
</origam-empty-state>

entry.name: TEmptyStateSize
entry.description_fallback: Vertical density of <OrigamEmptyState>. Drives icon size, title/description font sizes, gap and padding via per-size token overrides.
entry.definition:
/**
 * Vertical density of `<OrigamEmptyState>`. Drives icon size + title /
 * description font sizes + gap + padding via per-size token overrides.
 *
 * - `sm` — compact (inline empty zones inside dense containers).
 * - `md` — default (full-page-section empty states).
 * - `lg` — generous (hero empty pages, marketing-style placeholders).
 */
export type TEmptyStateSize = 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'

values.description_fallback: Compact — smaller icon, reduced font sizes, tighter padding. Suitable for inline empty zones inside dense containers such as sidebars or data tables.
Default — balanced icon size, standard font sizes. Suitable for full-page section empty states.
Generous — larger icon, bigger font sizes, more padding. Suitable for hero empty pages or marketing-style placeholders.

examples.title_fallback: Compact empty state for a data table

examples.code:
<origam-empty-state
  preset="no-data"
  title="Welcome aboard"
  description="Nothing here yet — create your first workspace."
  size="lg"
>
  <template #actions>
    <origam-btn color="primary" size="large">Get started</origam-btn>
  </template>
</origam-empty-state>

entry.name: TFile
entry.description_fallback: Model-value type for <OrigamFileField>. Accepts a single File object, an array of File objects, or null when no file is selected.
entry.definition:
/**
 * Model-value type accepted by `<OrigamFileField>`.
 *
 * - `File`         — single-file mode (`multiple` is false or absent).
 * - `Array<File>`  — multi-file mode (`multiple` is true).
 * - `null`         — no file selected / cleared state.
 */
export type TFile = Array<File> | File | null

values.description_fallback: A single browser File object — used when the multiple prop is false (default).
An array of File objects — used when the multiple prop is true.
No file is currently selected, or the selection has been cleared.

examples.code:
<script setup lang="ts">
import type { TFile } from 'origam'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const selectedFile = ref<TFile>(null)
</script>

<template>
  <origam-file-field v-model="selectedFile" label="Upload document" />
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import type { TFile } from 'origam'
import { ref } from 'vue'

const files = ref<TFile>(null)
</script>

<template>
  <origam-file-field v-model="files" multiple label="Upload images" />
</template>

entry.name: TFileFieldDisplay
entry.description_fallback: Controls how a multi-file selection is rendered inside <OrigamFileField>.
entry.definition:
/**
 * How a multi-file selection is rendered.
 *   - `'list'`    (default) — vertical card stack under the field.
 *   - `'chips'`   — each file as a closable `<OrigamChip>` inline.
 *   - `'counter'` — single "N files" line + an `<OrigamCounter>`.
 */
export type TFileFieldDisplay = 'list' | 'chips' | 'counter'

values.description_fallback: Default. Renders each selected file as a card in a vertical stack below the field. Suitable when filenames and per-file actions (remove, download, progress) need to be visible.
Renders each selected file as a closable <OrigamChip> inline inside the field. Compact mode — best for forms where many files can be selected but vertical space is limited.
Shows a single "N files selected" line with an <OrigamCounter>. Most compact mode — use when the file names are irrelevant to the user after selection.

entry.name: TFileSize
entry.description_fallback: Controls whether and how file sizes are displayed in <OrigamFileField>. Accepts a boolean toggle or a numeric unit divisor (1000 for SI units, 1024 for binary units).
entry.definition:
/**
 * Controls whether and how file sizes are rendered inside
 * `<OrigamFileField>`.
 *
 * - `false`  — do not show file sizes (default when `showSize` is omitted).
 * - `true`   — show file sizes using the default divisor (1000 / SI: KB, MB, GB).
 * - `1000`   — explicit SI units (KB = 1 000 bytes, MB = 1 000 000 bytes).
 * - `1024`   — binary / IEC units (KiB = 1 024 bytes, MiB = 1 048 576 bytes).
 */
export type TFileSize = boolean | 1000 | 1024

values.description_fallback: File sizes are not displayed. This is the default behaviour when the showSize prop is omitted.
File sizes are displayed using the SI divisor (1 000) — KB, MB, GB.
Explicit SI (decimal) units: 1 KB = 1 000 bytes, 1 MB = 1 000 000 bytes. Matches the marketing convention used by most storage providers.
Binary / IEC units: 1 KiB = 1 024 bytes, 1 MiB = 1 048 576 bytes. Traditional computing convention; more accurate for OS-reported file sizes.

examples.title_fallback: Show size with binary / IEC units

entry.name: TFlexDirection
entry.description_fallback: CSS flex-direction values accepted by <OrigamRow> and other flex containers. Drives the direction child items flow along the main axis.

values.description_fallback: Default. Items are laid out horizontally, left to right (in LTR contexts).
Items are stacked vertically, top to bottom.
Items flow horizontally in reverse — right to left in LTR contexts.
Items are stacked vertically in reverse — bottom to top.

examples.code:
<origam-row direction="row-reverse">
  <origam-col>Last item shown first</origam-col>
  <origam-col>Middle</origam-col>
  <origam-col>First item shown last</origam-col>
</origam-row>

entry.name: TGridAutoFlow
entry.description_fallback: Closed list of CSS grid-auto-flow values surfaced by OrigamGrid — controls how implicit tracks are filled when items overflow the explicit template.
entry.definition:
export type TGridAutoFlow =
    | 'row'
    | 'column'
    | 'row dense'
    | 'column dense'

values.description_fallback: Items fill each row in turn, adding new rows as needed (default browser behaviour).
Items fill each column in turn, adding new columns as needed.
Row-first flow with a backfill pass — smaller items are placed in earlier gaps, improving visual density at the cost of DOM order.
Column-first flow with a backfill pass — same density algorithm applied to column layout.

examples.title_fallback: Dense column packing to eliminate gaps

entry.name: TGridGapSize
entry.description_fallback: Named gap size tokens for OrigamGrid: xs, sm, md, lg, xl.
entry.definition:
export type TGridGapSize =
    | 'xs'
    | 'sm'
    | 'md'
    | 'lg'
    | 'xl'

values.description_fallback: Extra small gap — resolves to var(--origam-grid---gap-xs).
Small gap — resolves to var(--origam-grid---gap-sm).
Medium gap — default for most grid layouts.
Large gap — resolves to var(--origam-grid---gap-lg).
Extra large gap — resolves to var(--origam-grid---gap-xl).

entry.name: TGridPlaceContent
entry.description_fallback: Shorthand alignment for the full grid track set inside its container (align-content + justify-content) — mirrors the CSS Box Alignment matrix used by OrigamGrid.
entry.definition:
export type TGridPlaceContent =
    | 'start'
    | 'center'
    | 'end'
    | 'stretch'
    | 'space-between'
    | 'space-around'
    | 'space-evenly'

values.description_fallback: Packs all tracks against the start edge of the container.
Centers the track bundle in the available space.
Packs all tracks against the end edge of the container.
Stretches tracks to fill the container (default when tracks have auto sizing).
Equal space between tracks; no space at the edges.
Equal space around each track; half that space at the edges.
Equal space between every track and both edges.

examples.title_fallback: Center the entire grid vertically and horizontally

entry.name: TGridPlaceItems
entry.description_fallback: Shorthand alignment applied to every item within its grid cell (align-items + justify-items). baseline is excluded because it has no meaningful semantics when items span rows or columns.
entry.definition:
export type TGridPlaceItems =
    | 'start'
    | 'center'
    | 'end'
    | 'stretch'

values.description_fallback: Aligns each item to the start edge of its cell.
Centers each item within its cell on both axes.
Aligns each item to the end edge of its cell.
Stretches each item to fill its entire cell (default grid behaviour).

examples.title_fallback: Center all items in their cells

entry.name: TGridPlaceSelf
entry.description_fallback: Per-item override of cell alignment (align-self + justify-self) on a GridItem — same value set as TGridPlaceItems but applied to a single item rather than the entire grid.
entry.definition:
export type TGridPlaceSelf =
    | 'start'
    | 'center'
    | 'end'
    | 'stretch'

values.description_fallback: Aligns this item to the start edge of its cell, overriding the grid-level alignItems.
Centers this item within its cell on both axes.
Aligns this item to the end edge of its cell.
Stretches this item to fill the full cell dimensions.

examples.title_fallback: Override alignment on a single item

entry.name: THSVA
entry.description_fallback: Structured hue-saturation-value-alpha color object used internally by OrigamColorPicker as its canonical color model. All color format conversions (HEX, RGBA, HSLA) go through THSVA as the pivot.
entry.definition: export type THSVA = { h: number, s: number, v: number, a?: number }

examples.title_fallback: Controlling ColorPicker with an HSVA object
examples.code:
<script setup lang="ts">
import type { THSVA } from 'origam'
const color = ref<THSVA>({ h: 210, s: 0.8, v: 0.9, a: 1 })
</script>

<template>
  <origam-color-picker v-model:color-hsv="color" />
</template>

entry.name: TIcon
entry.description_fallback: Accepted value for any icon prop across the design system — a string glyph name, a multi-path SVG array, or a Vue component.
entry.definition:
export type TIcon =
    | string
    | Array<(string | [path: string, opacity: number])>
    | Component

examples.title_fallback: Multi-path SVG array with per-path opacity

entry.name: TInfiniteScrollSide
entry.description_fallback: Which end(s) of the scroll container trigger the load event in OrigamInfiniteScroll — start, end, or both simultaneously.

values.description_fallback: The load sentinel is placed at the top/left of the scroll container — useful for prepend-loading (e.g. chat history).
The load sentinel is placed at the bottom/right — standard "load more" pagination (default).
Sentinels at both ends — bidirectional scrolling. The load event payload includes which side triggered the load.

examples.code: {{ msg.text }}

entry.name: TIntent
entry.description_fallback: Semantic intent values: neutral, primary, secondary, ghost, success, warning, danger, info.
entry.definition:
export type TIntent =
    | 'neutral'
    | 'primary'
    | 'secondary'
    | 'ghost'
    | 'success'
    | 'warning'
    | 'danger'
    | 'info'

values.description_fallback: Neutral / no semantic color — inherits from surface tokens.
Primary brand color — maps to --origam-color__action--primary---* tokens.
Secondary accent color — a complementary brand rung.
Transparent / ghost surface — used for low-emphasis actions.
Positive confirmation intent (green ramp).
Caution / attention intent (amber ramp).
Destructive / error intent (red ramp).
Informational intent (blue ramp).

entry.name: TKbdVariant
entry.description_fallback: Visual style of the OrigamKbd keyboard key badge — filled, outlined, or tonal.
entry.definition: export type TKbdVariant = 'filled' | 'outlined' | 'tonal'

values.description_fallback: Solid background key badge — high visual weight, suitable for prominent key combos.
Border-only badge with transparent background — lightweight, inline-documentation style.
Subtle tinted background at reduced opacity — balanced midpoint between filled and outlined.

entry.name: TLines
entry.description_fallback: Number of lines of text a list item subtitle can occupy before being truncated — controls the vertical density of OrigamList and OrigamListItem.

values.description_fallback: Single-line item — subtitle clamps to 1 line. Most compact layout.
Two-line item — subtitle wraps to at most 2 lines. Default for rich lists.
Three-line item — subtitle wraps to at most 3 lines. Suitable for content-heavy lists.

examples.code:
<origam-list lines="two">
  <origam-list-item
    title="Design system"
    subtitle="A collection of reusable components guided by clear standards."
  />
  <origam-list-item
    title="Accessibility"
    subtitle="Built-in ARIA, focus management and keyboard navigation."
  />
</origam-list>

entry.name: TLoadingValue
entry.description_fallback: Loading prop value — boolean (on/off), number (percentage), or structured loader config object.

entry.name: TLocationStrategy
entry.description_fallback: Overlay positioning strategy: static or connected.

values.description_fallback: Static — overlay is positioned in the normal document flow.
Connected — overlay is anchored to an activator element via computed position.

entry.name: TMasonryAlign
entry.description_fallback: Vertical alignment of items inside a masonry column — top (Pinterest-style, default) or center. Only observable in the JS fallback path; the CSS-native masonry path always aligns to top.

values.description_fallback: Items are packed against the top of each column (default, Pinterest-style). Works in both CSS-native and JS fallback paths.
Column content is centred vertically inside the container height. Forces the JS fallback path because CSS-native masonry does not expose an equivalent alignment hook.

entry.name: TMode
entry.description_fallback: Display mode for BottomNav: horizontal, vertical, or shift.

values.description_fallback: Horizontal layout — icon and label side by side.
Vertical layout — icon above label.
Shift mode — active item expands, inactive items show icon only.

entry.name: TPosition
entry.description_fallback: CSS position value accepted by the position prop on many Origam components — maps directly to the CSS position property.

values.description_fallback: Default CSS flow position — top/right/bottom/left have no effect.
Positioned relative to its normal flow position — creates a new stacking context.
Positioned relative to the viewport — stays put during scroll. Used by AppBars and FABs.
Positioned relative to the nearest positioned ancestor — used for overlays and decorative elements.
Behaves as relative until a scroll threshold, then fixed — ideal for sticky table headers and section navs.

examples.title_fallback: Fixed toolbar that stays at the top during scroll
Sticky section header inside a scroll container

entry.name: TReferrerPolicy
entry.description_fallback: Enum-derived union of all standard Referrer-Policy header values accepted by the referrerpolicy prop on OrigamImg — controls what information is sent in the Referer header when the browser fetches the image.
entry.definition:
export type TReferrerPolicy = `${REFERRER_POLICY}`

// Where REFERRER_POLICY is:
export enum REFERRER_POLICY {
    NO_REFERRER                     = 'no-referrer',
    NO_REFERRER_WHEN_DOWNGRADE      = 'no-referrer-when-downgrade',
    ORIGIN                          = 'origin',
    ORIGIN_WHEN_CROSS_ORIGIN        = 'origin-when-cross-origin',
    SAME_ORIGIN                     = 'same-origin',
    STRICT_ORIGIN                   = 'strict-origin',
    STRICT_ORIGIN_WHEN_CROSS_ORIGIN = 'strict-origin-when-cross-origin',
    UNSAFE_URL                      = 'unsafe-url'
}

values.description_fallback: No Referer header is sent with any request — maximum privacy.
Full URL sent on same-protocol requests; no header on HTTPS→HTTP downgrades (browser default).
Sends only the origin (scheme + host + port) without the path.
Full URL for same-origin requests; origin only for cross-origin.
Full URL for same-origin requests; no header for cross-origin.
Origin only on same-protocol requests; no header on HTTPS→HTTP downgrades.
Full URL for same-origin; origin only for cross-origin same-protocol; nothing on downgrade. Recommended default.
Full URL always sent — exposes path and query string to third parties, even over HTTP.

examples.title_fallback: Strict referrer policy on a cross-origin image

entry.name: TRounded
entry.description_fallback: Corner-radius variant tokens — x-small through x-large plus shaped and shaped-invert.
entry.definition:
export type TRounded = `${ROUNDED}`

// Where ROUNDED is:
export enum ROUNDED {
    X_SMALL      = 'x-small',
    SMALL        = 'small',
    DEFAULT      = 'default',
    MEDIUM       = 'medium',
    LARGE        = 'large',
    X_LARGE      = 'x-large',
    SHAPED       = 'shaped',
    SHAPED_INVERT = 'shaped-invert'
}

values.description_fallback: Extra small corner radius (2px).
Small corner radius (4px).
Default corner radius defined by the component token.
Medium corner radius (8px).
Large corner radius (12px).
Extra large corner radius (16px).
Pill-like shape — fully rounded left/right ends.
Inverted pill shape — concave corners on the ends.

entry.name: RouteLocationRaw
entry.description_fallback: Vue Router route destination — string path, location object or named route descriptor.

entry.name: TScrollStrategy
entry.description_fallback: Behaviour of a floating element (Overlay, Dialog, Tooltip, Menu) when the user scrolls the page while it is open — none, close, block or reposition.

values.description_fallback: Scroll events have no effect — the overlay stays open and in place regardless of page scroll.
Scrolling closes the overlay immediately — used by menus and tooltips where stale positioning would confuse the user.
Page scroll is prevented while the overlay is open — used by modal dialogs and full-screen drawers.
The overlay repositions itself to stay anchored to its activator as the page scrolls — used by anchored menus and select dropdowns.

examples.title_fallback: Dialog blocking page scroll while open
Menu that repositions when the page scrolls
examples.code:
<origam-menu scroll-strategy="reposition">
  <template #activator="{ props }">
    <origam-btn v-bind="props">Open Menu</origam-btn>
  </template>
  <origam-list>
    <origam-list-item title="Option A" />
    <origam-list-item title="Option B" />
  </origam-list>
</origam-menu>

entry.name: TSelectItemKey
entry.description_fallback: Flexible item-key resolver for list/select components — boolean, string, array path or function.
entry.definition:
export type TSelectItemKey<T = Record<string, any>> =
    | boolean | null | undefined  // Ignored
    | string                       // Lookup by key, supports dot notation
    | Readonly<Array<(string | number)>> // Nested lookup, each item is a key
    | ((item: T, fallback?: any) => any)

entry.name: TSize
entry.description_fallback: Component size scale: x-small, small, default, large, x-large.

values.description_fallback: Extra small — smallest token size rung.
Small — reduced footprint for inline contexts.
Default — the baseline size for the component.
Large — increased surface area.
Extra large — maximum size rung.

entry.name: TSkeletonVariant
entry.description_fallback: Shape preset for OrigamSkeleton loading placeholders — selects a predefined animated outline that mirrors the content it replaces.
entry.definition: export type TSkeletonVariant = 'text' | 'rectangular' | 'circular' | 'card' | 'list-item'

values.description_fallback: Short horizontal bar — placeholder for a line of text, title, or label.
Solid rectangle — placeholder for images, video thumbnails, or block content.
Circle — placeholder for avatars, icon buttons, or badge shapes.
Card-shaped composite placeholder with a rectangular media area and stacked text lines below.
List item composite — avatar circle on the left with two text lines on the right.

examples.code:
<origam-skeleton variant="text" width="200px" />
<origam-skeleton variant="rectangular" width="100%" height="180px" />
<origam-skeleton variant="circular" width="48px" height="48px" />
<origam-skeleton variant="card" />
<origam-skeleton variant="list-item" />

entry.name: TSliderFieldVariant
entry.description_fallback: Visual and behavioural variant of OrigamSliderField — full form-field chrome, bare video-scrubber, or waveform-backed audio scrubber.

values.description_fallback: Default variant — full OrigamInput chrome with label, helper text, prepend/append slots and validation messages. Used in settings panels and form-grade sliders.
Bare scrubber without input chrome — 2 px hairline rail at rest, 4 px on hover/focus, thumb hidden until hover. Used by OrigamMediaController as a video timeline.
Same as timer plus a waveform background painted from the peaks prop. Used by OrigamAudio as its waveform scrubber.

examples.title_fallback: Form-grade slider with label and validation
Bare scrubber for a media controller

entry.name: TSnackbarGroupDirection
entry.description_fallback: Stacking direction for OrigamSnackbarGroup — controls whether new items are appended at the top or bottom of the visible list.
entry.definition: export type TSnackbarGroupDirection = 'top-down' | 'bottom-up'

values.description_fallback: Newest item appended at the bottom of the visible list; older items drift upward as the stack grows. Default for top-anchored locations.
Newest item appears at the top of the visible list; older items drift downward. Default for bottom-anchored locations.

examples.title_fallback: Snackbar group stacking bottom-up at top-right

entry.name: TSnackbarGroupLocation
entry.description_fallback: Viewport anchor location for OrigamSnackbarGroup — two-keyword strings describe a corner, single keywords describe an edge with horizontal centering.
entry.definition:
export type TSnackbarGroupLocation =
    | 'top-left'
    | 'top-right'
    | 'top-center'
    | 'bottom-left'
    | 'bottom-right'
    | 'bottom-center'
    | 'top'
    | 'bottom'

values.description_fallback: Pins the stack to the top-left corner of the viewport.
Pins the stack to the top-right corner of the viewport.
Centers the stack horizontally at the top of the viewport.
Pins the stack to the bottom-left corner of the viewport.
Pins the stack to the bottom-right corner of the viewport.
Centers the stack horizontally at the bottom of the viewport.
Alias for top-center — horizontal centering at the top edge.
Alias for bottom-center — horizontal centering at the bottom edge.

examples.code:
<origam-snackbar-group location="bottom-right">
  <origam-snackbar text="File saved successfully" />
  <origam-snackbar text="Sync complete" color="success" />
</origam-snackbar-group>

entry.name: TStatus
entry.description_fallback: Feedback status values: success, info, warning, error.

values.description_fallback: Positive confirmation — maps to the success semantic token ramp.
Informational message — maps to the info semantic token ramp.
Caution message — maps to the warning semantic token ramp.
Error / failure state — maps to the error / danger token ramp.

examples.code:
<origam-alert status="success">Saved successfully.</origam-alert>
<origam-alert status="info">New version available.</origam-alert>
<origam-alert status="warning">Check your input.</origam-alert>
<origam-alert status="error">Something went wrong.</origam-alert>

entry.name: TStepperItemStatus
entry.description_fallback: Lifecycle status of a single step inside OrigamStepper — drives the step icon, colour and interactive affordances.
entry.definition: export type TStepperItemStatus = 'pending' | 'active' | 'done' | 'error'

values.description_fallback: Step has not been visited yet. Renders with a neutral style and is not interactive unless the stepper is non-linear.
Currently focused step. The header is highlighted and the step content panel is visible.
Step has been completed. A checkmark icon replaces the step number and the step is navigable to in non-linear mode.
Step completed with a validation error. Renders with the danger intent colour and a warning icon.

examples.code:
<origam-stepper>
  <origam-stepper-item title="Account" status="done" />
  <origam-stepper-item title="Profile" status="active" />
  <origam-stepper-item title="Review" status="pending" />
  <origam-stepper-item title="Payment" status="error" />
</origam-stepper>

entry.name: TTextMaskAnimation
entry.description_fallback: Animation strategy applied to the background painted under the background-clip: text mask in OrigamTextMask. Every strategy respects prefers-reduced-motion.
entry.definition:
export type TTextMaskAnimation =
    | 'pan'
    | 'rotate'
    | 'pulse'
    | 'zoom'

values.description_fallback: Translates the gradient horizontally in a continuous loop. Default. Best fit for long horizontal linear-gradient ramps.
Rotates the background-image continuously. Best fit for conic gradients where every angle is meaningful.
Scales 1 to 1.2 and back to 1 — a subtle breathing effect that keeps the gradient visible throughout.
Continuous scale from 1 to 2 — a cinematic moving-forward feel that progressively reveals finer gradient detail.

entry.name: TTextareaMode
entry.description_fallback: Render mode for OrigamTextareaField — plain keeps the legacy textarea element while rich swaps it for a contenteditable surface with formatting support.

values.description_fallback: Keeps the legacy <textarea> element. The v-model contract is a raw string. No toolbar is rendered.
Swaps the textarea for a contenteditable surface wrapped by the Field shell. The v-model becomes sanitised HTML or Markdown depending on the output prop.

entry.name: TTextareaOutput
entry.description_fallback: Output format of the rich-text textarea — controls how the editor DOM is serialised back into the v-model string.

values.description_fallback: Emits sanitised HTML (allowlist on tags, attributes and URL schemes). Preserves rich formatting such as bold, italic, links and lists.
Emits a CommonMark-flavoured subset (bold, italic, links, lists, headings, code). The <u> tag is preserved verbatim because vanilla Markdown has no underline syntax.

entry.name: TTextareaToolbarCommand
entry.description_fallback: Identifiers for every rich-text toolbar command — drives the toolbar contract, the format emit payload and the keyboard shortcut map.
entry.definition:
export type TTextareaToolbarCommand = `${TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND}`

// Where TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND is:
export enum TEXTAREA_TOOLBAR_COMMAND {
    BOLD          = 'bold',
    ITALIC        = 'italic',
    UNDERLINE     = 'underline',
    LINK          = 'link',
    LIST_BULLET   = 'list-bullet',
    LIST_ORDERED  = 'list-ordered',
    HEADING_1     = 'heading-1',
    HEADING_2     = 'heading-2',
    HEADING_3     = 'heading-3',
    HEADING       = 'heading',
    CODE_INLINE   = 'code-inline',
    CLEAR_FORMAT  = 'clear-format'
}

values.description_fallback: Toggles bold on the current selection. Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl/Cmd+B.
Toggles italic on the current selection. Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl/Cmd+I.
Toggles underline on the current selection. Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl/Cmd+U.
Opens the link editor for the current selection — inserts or edits an anchor tag.
Wraps the current block in an unordered list item.
Wraps the current block in an ordered (numbered) list item.
Applies H1 block format to the current paragraph.
Applies H2 block format to the current paragraph.
Applies H3 block format to the current paragraph.
Generic heading command — cycles or opens a heading picker depending on toolbar configuration.
Wraps the selection in a <code> span for inline monospace formatting.
Strips all inline formatting (bold, italic, underline, link, code) from the current selection.

examples.title_fallback: Custom toolbar with a subset of commands

entry.name: TTextareaToolbarPosition
entry.description_fallback: Where the rich-text toolbar is rendered relative to the editable surface.

values.description_fallback: Toolbar is placed above the editable surface, inside the Field shell.
Toolbar is placed below the editable surface, inside the Field shell.
Toolbar uses position: sticky inside the Field shell so it stays visible as the editable surface scrolls.

examples.title_fallback: Floating toolbar that stays visible on scroll

entry.name: TTheme
entry.description_fallback: Runtime brand name applied to the <html data-theme="…"> attribute or to a sub-tree via OrigamThemeProvider. Orthogonal to the light/dark mode axis.
entry.definition: export type TTheme = 'auto' | 'light' | 'dark' | (string & {})

values.description_fallback: No data-theme attribute is set. Children resolve against the closest ancestor theme, or the default brand at the document root.
Legacy alias kept for backwards compatibility — was the default brand before the two-axis theming model.
Legacy alias kept for backwards compatibility. As of the two-axis model, prefer setting mode="dark" on OrigamThemeProvider.
Any other string is treated as a custom brand theme (e.g. "sobre", "brand-a"). The matching CSS token file must be loaded by the consumer.

examples.title_fallback: Sub-tree with a custom brand theme
examples.code:
<origam-theme-provider theme="brand-a">
  <origam-card>This card renders with the brand-a token set.</origam-card>
</origam-theme-provider>

entry.name: TTimelineOrientation
entry.description_fallback: Layout axis of the OrigamTimeline — vertical (top-to-bottom) or horizontal (left-to-right).
entry.definition: export type TTimelineOrientation = 'vertical' | 'horizontal'

values.description_fallback: Events are stacked from top to bottom along the Y axis. Default orientation.
Events are laid out left to right along the X axis. Useful for timelines that span calendar periods or process steps.

entry.name: TTransitionMode
entry.description_fallback: Controls the sequencing of enter and leave transitions when the content inside OrigamTransition changes.

values.description_fallback: The new element transitions IN first, then the leaving element transitions OUT. Useful when content should appear before the old content disappears.
The leaving element transitions OUT first, then the new element transitions IN. The most common mode for page-level route transitions.
Enter and leave transitions run simultaneously. Matches Vue's native <Transition> default behaviour.

examples.title_fallback: Out-in mode for a route-level swap

entry.name: TTransitionMode
entry.description_fallback: Vue transition mode: in-out, out-in, or default (simultaneous).

values.description_fallback: New element transitions in first, then old element transitions out.
Old element transitions out first, then new element transitions in.
Simultaneous — both transitions run at the same time (Vue default).

entry.name: TTreeviewSelectMode
entry.description_fallback: Selection cardinality of the OrigamTreeview — controls whether zero, one or many nodes can be selected at the same time.
entry.definition: export type TTreeviewSelectMode = 'single' | 'multiple' | 'none'

values.description_fallback: Only one node can be selected at a time. Selecting a new node automatically deselects the previous one.
Multiple nodes can be selected simultaneously. Paired with checkboxes when selectableNodes is set.
Selection is disabled. Nodes can still be expanded/collapsed but clicking them produces no selection state change.

entry.name: TTreeviewSelectableNodes
entry.description_fallback: Which nodes in the OrigamTreeview can receive a selected state — only leaf nodes or every node in the tree.
entry.definition: export type TTreeviewSelectableNodes = 'leaf' | 'all'

values.description_fallback: Only leaf nodes (nodes with no children) can be selected. Parent nodes act solely as expand/collapse toggles.
Both parent and leaf nodes can be selected. Selecting a parent in multiple mode propagates the state to its children (cascade).

entry.name: TValidateOn
entry.description_fallback: Controls when field-level validation rules are evaluated — on blur, on input, on form submit, or a lazy variant of each.
entry.definition:
export type TValidateOn = `${VALIDATE_ON}`

// Where VALIDATE_ON is:
export enum VALIDATE_ON {
    BLUR         = 'blur',
    INPUT        = 'input',
    SUBMIT       = 'submit',
    LAZY_BLUR    = 'lazy blur',
    LAZY_INPUT   = 'lazy input',
    LAZY_SUBMIT  = 'lazy submit',
    BLUR_LAZY    = 'blur lazy',
    INPUT_LAZY   = 'input lazy',
    SUBMIT_LAZY  = 'submit lazy',
    LAZY         = 'lazy',
}

values.description_fallback: Validation runs when the field loses focus.
Validation runs on every keystroke / value change — immediate inline feedback.
Validation runs only when the enclosing form is submitted.
First validation fires on blur; subsequent validations fire on every input after the first error was shown.
First validation fires on input; once an error appears it continues on every subsequent change.
First validation fires on submit; after that errors are cleared on every input change.
Alias of "lazy blur" — validate on blur with lazy re-evaluation once an error is visible.
Alias of "lazy input".
Alias of "lazy submit".
Defers the first validation to the next cycle that matches the default trigger (blur), then validates eagerly on every subsequent change.

examples.title_fallback: Validate an email field on blur with lazy re-check

entry.name: TVariant
entry.description_fallback: Visual surface variant for elevation-driven components: text, flat, elevated, tonal, outlined, plain, ghost.
entry.definition:
export type TVariant = `${VARIANT}`

// Where VARIANT is:
export enum VARIANT {
    TEXT     = 'text',
    FLAT     = 'flat',
    ELEVATED = 'elevated',
    TONAL    = 'tonal',
    OUTLINED = 'outlined',
    PLAIN    = 'plain',
    GHOST    = 'ghost'
}

values.description_fallback: Text-only — no background, no border.
Flat filled background, no elevation shadow.
Filled background with box-shadow elevation.
Subtle tonal fill derived from the current color intent.
Border outline only — no fill.
Plain — minimal styling, blends into any surface.
Glassmorphism — translucent tint with backdrop-filter blur.

examples.code:
<origam-btn variant="elevated" color="primary">Elevated</origam-btn>
<origam-btn variant="tonal"    color="primary">Tonal</origam-btn>
<origam-btn variant="outlined" color="primary">Outlined</origam-btn>
<origam-btn variant="text"     color="primary">Text</origam-btn>
<origam-btn variant="ghost"    color="primary">Ghost</origam-btn>

entry.name: TVideoControls
entry.description_fallback: Controls rendering strategy for OrigamVideo — custom Origam UI, native browser controls, or no controls at all.
entry.definition: export type TVideoControls = 'custom' | 'native' | 'none'

values.description_fallback: The component paints its own toolbar with play/pause, scrubber, volume, captions, PIP and fullscreen buttons. The <video> element is rendered WITHOUT the native controls attribute. Default.
The <video> element receives the native controls attribute and the browser paints its own toolbar. Useful for AirPlay on Safari or inside Tauri shells that talk to native players.
Neither the custom nor the native toolbar is rendered. The consumer drives playback programmatically through the #controls slot or the exposed composable methods.
